didYouKnow: Walt Disney was fired from a newspaper for 'lacking imagination.' He went bankrupt before Mickey Mouse.
Steven Spielberg was rejected from USC film school three times. He eventually got an honorary degree from them.
J.K. Rowling was a single mother on welfare when she wrote Harry Potter. Twelve publishers rejected it.
Reid Hoffman launched LinkedIn from his living room after multiple failed startups. He says 'an entrepreneur is someone who jumps off a cliff and builds a plane on the way down.'
Sara Blakely spent two years getting rejected by every hosiery manufacturer before Spanx took off. She had zero fashion industry experience.
James Dyson built 5,127 failed prototypes before his vacuum worked. It took him 15 years.
Arianna Huffington's second book was rejected by 36 publishers. She went on to build The Huffington Post.
Howard Schultz was turned down by 242 investors when trying to fund Starbucks.
Oprah was fired from her first TV job and told she was 'unfit for television.'
Steve Jobs was fired from Apple — the company he founded. He called it 'the best thing that could have ever happened to me.'
